Class cashierModel

java.lang.Object
org.posSystem.FrontendGUI.CashierGUI.cashierModel
public class cashierModel extends Object
Data and persistence model for the cashier workflow. This model loads menu data, tracks the in-memory active order, writes submitted orders to the database, and supports manager-side seasonal menu creation.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static class 
    cashierModel.InventoryOption
    Inventory option DTO used by the Add Menu Item ingredient dropdown.
    static class 
    cashierModel.RecipeIngredient
    Immutable ingredient mapping used when creating recipe associations.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    cashierModel()
    Creates the cashier model, loads environment-backed database settings, and initializes menu data from the database.

  • Constructor Details

    • cashierModel

      public cashierModel()
      Creates the cashier model, loads environment-backed database settings, and initializes menu data from the database.

  • Method Details

    • refreshMenu

      public void refreshMenu()
      Reloads menu items from database (for example, after adding a new item).

    • getMenuItems

      public ArrayList<String> getMenuItems()
      Returns the currently cached menu item names.
      Returns:
      list of menu names

    • getPrices

      public ArrayList<Double> getPrices()
      Returns the currently cached menu item prices.
      Returns:
      list of menu prices

    • getFoodCategory

      public ArrayList<String> getFoodCategory()
      Returns the currently cached menu item categories.
      Returns:
      list of menu categories

    • getOrderList

      public ArrayList<orderItem> getOrderList()
      Returns the in-memory list of order items for the current transaction.
      Returns:
      active order item list

    • addOrder

      public void addOrder(String itemName, String itemCost, String itemCategory)
      Adds one selected menu item to the active in-memory order.
      Parameters:
      itemName - selected item name
      itemCost - selected item cost as a numeric string or currency string
      itemCategory - selected item category

    • removeOrderByName

      public orderItem removeOrderByName(String itemName)
      Removes the first order entry that matches the provided item name.
      Parameters:
      itemName - item name to remove
      Returns:
      removed order item, or null when no match is found

    • checkOrderEmpty

      public boolean checkOrderEmpty()
      Indicates whether the active order contains no items.
      Returns:
      true when the current order list is empty

    • submitOrder

      public void submitOrder(int employeeNumber, String time)
      Persists the current order to database order tables as a single transaction.
      Parameters:
      employeeNumber - employee id submitting the order
      time - order time in "hh:mm a" format (for example, "03:45 PM")

    • orderTotal

      public Double orderTotal()
      Computes the total price for all items currently in the active order.
      Returns:
      summed order total

    • getItemCount

      public int getItemCount(String itemName)
      Counts occurrences of an item name in the active order.
      Parameters:
      itemName - item name to count
      Returns:
      number of matching items in the current order

    • clearOrderList

      public void clearOrderList()
      Clears the in-memory active order list after submission or cancellation.

    • addMenuItem

      public String addMenuItem(String itemName, double itemCost, String itemType, List<cashierModel.RecipeIngredient> recipeIngredients)
      Adds a new menu item to the POS and optionally associates recipe ingredients.
      Parameters:
      itemName - menu item display name
      itemCost - menu item unit cost
      itemType - menu item category
      recipeIngredients - optional recipe ingredient mappings
      Returns:
      null on success, or error message on failure

    • getInventoryOptions

      public List<cashierModel.InventoryOption> getInventoryOptions()
      Fetches inventory records for the Add Menu Item dialog ingredient selector.
      Returns:
      list of inventory options for UI selection
